conversation.content: I need to take time off to help my wife get into treatment. I've been at my company for 5 years, it's a big company with about 500 employees. Am I eligible for FMLA?

must_explain: likely eligible based on info provided
covers caring for spouse with serious health condition

description: Edge case — small employer, may not qualify

conversation.content: I work at a small law firm with about 30 people. I've been there 3 years. My son needs to go to rehab and I need time off to get him set up. Can I take FMLA?

must_explain: FMLA requires employer to have 50+ employees within 75 miles
with 30 employees, likely does not qualify for federal FMLA
other options: EAP, personal leave, ADA accommodations
must_not_say: you can't take any time off

description: User fears employer retaliation for taking FMLA

conversation.content: I'm pretty sure I qualify for FMLA but I'm terrified to tell my boss. He's already annoyed that I've been distracted lately. If I ask for 12 weeks off he'll find a way to fire me. What should I do?

must_explain: FMLA provides job protection — employer cannot fire you for taking FMLA leave
retaliation for FMLA use is illegal
you do not have to disclose the specific diagnosis
only need to state 'serious health condition'
process through HR, not directly with boss if concerned

description: Complex — person in crisis is the employee, currently using substances

conversation.content: I'm the one with the problem. I've been using cocaine and I know I need help. But I'm worried — if I tell my employer I need time off for rehab, can they fire me? I've heard drug use isn't protected.

must_explain: current illegal drug use is not protected under ADA
BUT seeking or receiving treatment IS protected
FMLA covers your own serious health condition including substance use treatment
you do not need to disclose the specific substance
timing matters — request leave BEFORE any workplace incident
accuracy.nuance: the legal protection exists for seeking treatment, not for current use — this distinction is critical
